instructions: Review entries, then set include_on_site to true. Run: python scripts/generate_html.py

vulnerabilities.title: Google Chromium V8
vulnerabilities.name: Google Chromium V8 Improper Restriction of Operations Within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: Google Chromium V8 contains an improper restriction of operations within the bounds of a memory buffer vulnerability that could allow a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code inside a sandbox via a crafted HTML page. This vulnerability could affect multiple web browsers that utilize Chromium, including, but not limited to, Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, and Opera.
vulnerabilities.short_description: Google Chromium V8 contains an improper restriction of operations within the bounds of a memory buffer vulnerability...
vulnerabilities.fix: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

vulnerabilities.title: Google Skia
vulnerabilities.name: Google Skia Out-of-Bounds Write Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: Google Skia contains an out-of-bounds write vulnerability that could allow a remote attacker to perform out of bounds memory access via a crafted HTML page. This vulnerability affects Google Chrome and ChromeOS, Android, Flutter, and possibly other products.
vulnerabilities.cisaNotes: This vulnerability affects a common open-source component, third-party library, or a protocol used by different products. Please check with specific vendors for information on patching status. For more information, please see: https://chromereleases.googleblog.com/2026/03/stable-channel-update-for-desktop_12.html#:~:text=Google%20is%20aware ; https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-3909

vulnerabilities.fix: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

vulnerabilities.title: Rockwell Multiple Products
vulnerabilities.name: Rockwell Multiple Products Insufficient Protected Credentials Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: Multiple Rockwell products contain an insufficient protected credentials vulnerability. Studio 5000 Logix Designer software may allow a key to be discovered. This key is used to verify Logix controllers are communicating with Rockwell Automation design software. If successfully exploited, this vulnerability could allow an unauthorized application to connect with Logix controllers. To leverage this vulnerability, an unauthorized user would require network access to the controller.
vulnerabilities.short_description: Multiple Rockwell products contain an insufficient protected credentials vulnerability. Studio 5000 Logix Designer...
vulnerabilities.fix: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.
